approach: Generated one companion Jupyter notebook per chapter from fenced Python code blocks. Blocks marked noexec are preserved as notebook markdown for readers but are not executed. Runnable blocks execute sequentially in the chapter notebook, capture stdout and generated image files, and inject captured outputs back into chapter.md under a reproducible-results section. The book now points readers to the official NeqSim documentation at https://equinor.github.io/neqsim/, includes a fully expanded generic integrated offshore process-model chapter with all 49 source notebook code cells, and adds a process optimization chapter grounded in the documented optimizer stack and repository examples. The learning architecture now includes a front-matter roadmap, a mastery loop, reader paths, a competency matrix, and a back-matter capstone portfolio rubric.
conclusions: The process_modeling_with_neqsim_python_2026 book now has generated companion notebooks for every chapter that contains Python examples. Chapter 15 has been rewritten as a generic detailed walkthrough of the full advanced process-model notebook, including all source code cells, section-by-section explanations, and end-of-notebook reporting to results.json plus .neqsim and .xip packages. Chapter 15 also includes a separate anonymous runnable notebook with embedded synthetic inputs and a concise expected-main-output-by-cell table; it runs without external spreadsheets, private helper modules, absolute paths, or asset-specific names and writes results.json, .neqsim, lifecycle-state JSON, .xip artifacts, and two real figures generated from the simulated product flows and compressor powers. Those Chapter 15 figures are included directly in the book and recorded in results.json with captions and discussion metadata. Chapter 15 now maps its major operations and topics back to earlier book chapters and official NeqSim documentation, with Chapter 12 extended to document .neqsim archives and .xip review bundles before they are used in the worked example. The overall book structure has been reviewed and tightened so Part V reads as a deliberate capstone path: integrated model, reusable example library, then optimization and debottlenecking. The process optimization chapter executed successfully and includes the expected throughput table and plot. Captured notebook results are inserted before exercises to preserve chapter structure, and duplicate transient notebook image outputs are suppressed in favor of stable saved figure files.
